benchmark_aplicado: Servicio local (salud, legal) — SEO: 55, UX/UI: 60, Marketing: 60, Contenido: 65, Tecnico: 50. Applied because this is a local service business (handyman) serving a specific geographic area (Central Pinellas County, FL). The benchmark for local service providers emphasizes trust signals, local SEO, and clear contact information over advanced technical features.

positivos: H1 contains primary keyword 'Home Handyman Repair Services' with geographic targeting (Largo, Clearwater, Seminole)
Meta title present and keyword-rich with service and location terms
CTAs use descriptive action verbs: 'Schedule Now', 'Contact me today' rather than generic 'Click here'
Schema.org LocalBusiness structured data implemented with address and geo coordinates
negativos: Meta description is completely missing — critical for search snippet optimization and CTR
Most images lack proper descriptive alt text; gallery images have generic 'Handyman project completed' alt tags
No heading hierarchy detected — multiple sections appear to use similar heading levels without H1>H2>H3 structure
No favicon detected, reducing brand recognition in browser tabs and bookmarks
Keyword stuffing in meta title with repetitive 'Handyman' variations

recomendaciones.accion: Add a compelling meta description (150-160 chars) targeting 'handyman services Largo FL' with a call to action
Write unique, descriptive alt text for all gallery and section images describing the specific work shown
Implement proper heading hierarchy: single H1 per page, H2 for sections, H3 for subsections
Add favicon using the brand orange color for browser tab recognition
Create individual service pages (e.g., /carpentry, /plumbing) for better keyword targeting

positivos: Consistent color palette with orange (#F59402) as primary accent and dark backgrounds for contrast sections
Readable typography with Quicksand for headings and Muli for body text — both clean sans-serif fonts
Clear navigation with 6 logically organized menu items covering key pages
negativos: GoDaddy template lacks distinctive visual identity — looks generic for a handyman business
Gallery section shows 29 images in a carousel without categorization, making it hard to find relevant work examples
No visual differentiation between service categories on the services page — all listed as plain text
White (#FFFFFF) background with near-black (#1B1B1B) text creates harsh contrast; no warm tones to match the friendly brand personality
Hero section heading repeated 5 times (carousel artifact), creating a cluttered experience

recomendaciones.accion: Replace pure white background with a warm off-white (#FAFAF8 or similar) to soften the look and match the friendly brand tone
Organize gallery into categories (carpentry, plumbing, electrical, etc.) with before/after pairs where possible
Design service cards with icons and clear visual hierarchy instead of plain text list
Fix hero carousel to display content cleanly without repeated headings

positivos: Strong value proposition: 'family-owned, 30+ years experience, reasonable price' addresses core customer concerns (trust, experience, affordability)
Primary CTA 'Schedule Now' is visible in hero and links directly to contact form — clear conversion path
Testimonials section ('Raving Reviews') present as social proof element
Transparency as a selling point: 'What We Don't Do' page builds trust by showing honesty about limitations
negativos: Testimonial section appears to be present but actual review content was not extractable — may be poorly implemented or empty
No specific numbers or quantified results (e.g., '500+ projects completed', '4.9 star rating')
No urgency or scarcity elements to encourage immediate action
Missing before/after project showcases which are the most compelling content for handyman services
No mention of licensing, insurance, or certifications which are key trust factors for home service providers

recomendaciones.accion: Add 3-5 real customer testimonials with names, locations, and specific project descriptions
Include trust badges: licensed, insured, years in business, number of projects completed
Create before/after photo pairs for the most impressive projects to showcase quality
Add a 'Free Quote' badge or banner element near the hero to reinforce the no-cost consultation offer

positivos: Service descriptions are written in first person by the owner (Ryan), creating a personal and authentic tone
Complete contact information: phone, email, physical address, and business hours all present
Comprehensive service area listing with specific cities and neighborhoods in Pinellas County
Clear scope definition with both 'What We Do' and 'What We Don't Do' pages reducing misaligned inquiries
negativos: Service descriptions are very brief (1-2 sentences each) and lack detail about process, materials, or what to expect
No FAQ section addressing common customer questions (pricing range, timeline, booking process)
No blog or educational content that could drive organic search traffic
The 'About' section lacks a personal photo of Ryan or the team, missing the personal connection that's key for in-home services
Repeated service area text appears in multiple sections (hero, about, CTA) creating redundancy

recomendaciones.accion: Expand each service description to 3-4 sentences covering what's included, typical timeline, and why customers choose ATH for that service
Add an FAQ section with 8-10 common questions about pricing, scheduling, service area, and process
Include a personal photo and brief bio of Ryan on the about page to build personal trust
Consolidate service area mentions to avoid repetition; list once prominently with a service area map

positivos: Schema.org LocalBusiness structured data implemented for search engine understanding
Google Analytics and Facebook Pixel tracking installed for visitor and campaign analytics
reCAPTCHA integration on contact form to prevent spam submissions
negativos: Built on GoDaddy Website Builder — limited customization, poor performance, and restricted SEO capabilities
No social media profiles linked despite having Facebook Pixel installed
Service Worker registered but unclear benefit for a simple service business website
No evidence of image optimization or lazy loading strategy — 29 gallery images could significantly impact load time
Template-based platform creates code bloat with unnecessary CSS/JS for features not used

recomendaciones.accion: Migrate from GoDaddy to a lightweight custom-built site (HTML + Tailwind CSS) for faster load times and full control
Implement proper image optimization: WebP format, lazy loading, and responsive srcset for gallery images
Set up Google Business Profile integration and link social media accounts
Add Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags for better social media sharing

tecnico.stack_actual: GoDaddy Website Builder with Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, reCAPTCHA, Service Worker
tecnico.stack_recomendado: Static HTML + Tailwind CSS + Alpine.js — lightweight, fast, and fully customizable for a local service business

quick_wins: Add meta description targeting 'handyman services Largo FL' with a compelling call to action
Add 3-5 real customer testimonials with names and project details to the reviews section
Include trust badges: licensed, insured, 30+ years experience, service guarantee
Fix hero section to display cleanly without repeated heading elements
Add descriptive alt text to all gallery images for SEO and accessibility

semana_1: Add meta description and fix meta title to remove keyword stuffing
Write descriptive alt text for all images
Add 3-5 customer testimonials with real names and project descriptions
Include trust badges (licensed, insured, years experience) in hero or features section
mes_1: Migrate site from GoDaddy to custom HTML + Tailwind CSS build
Redesign service section with visual cards, icons, and expanded descriptions
Create FAQ section with 8-10 common customer questions
Organize gallery into categorized before/after showcases
Implement proper heading hierarchy and image optimization
Add personal photo and expanded bio for owner Ryan
largo_plazo: Create individual service landing pages for top services (carpentry, plumbing, electrical)
Set up and link social media profiles (Google Business, Facebook, Instagram)
Start a maintenance tips blog for organic search traffic
Add Open Graph meta tags and service area map

resumen_ejecutivo: The current GoDaddy-built website has solid content foundations with authentic service descriptions and complete contact info, but suffers from a generic template appearance, missing meta description, and poor technical performance that likely hurts search visibility against local competitors. The biggest opportunity is migrating to a custom lightweight build that showcases Ryan's 30+ years of experience with compelling before/after work galleries, real testimonials, and trust signals that convert visitors into quote requests. The immediate next step is building a professional, fast-loading custom site that leverages the existing strong content while dramatically improving SEO, visual design, and conversion-focused marketing elements.

extras: Alpine.js for interactive gallery and mobile menu
Formspree or Netlify Forms for contact form
stack_para_web.justificacion: Low complexity local service business with static content. HTML + Tailwind + Alpine.js provides the fastest load times, full SEO control, and zero framework overhead — ideal for a handyman service that needs to rank locally and convert mobile visitors quickly.
